name: regime_crossover_nu_q6
description: The keystone 'best tool for the job' figure: relative L2 error of CH vs QLBM as a function of viscosity nu, at fixed q=6. The two methods cross between nu=0.02 and nu=0.03 -- CH wins the low-nu / sharp regime, QLBM wins the high-nu / diffusive regime. Mirror failure modes: CH degrades monotonically as nu rises; QLBM collapses to signal-level error (relL2 ~ 1) at low nu then becomes accurate as diffusion grows. Fixed q isolates the REGIME axis (the cost-scaling and dial-up figures cover the q axis). Source: burgers_ab_mutual_q6_nu{015,020,030,050}; relL2 = RMS(method_final - ftcs_final)/RMS(ftcs_final), method grid interpolated onto the reference.
_takeaways: Crossover between nu=0.02 and nu=0.03: pick CH below it, QLBM above it.
CH error rises monotonically with nu (0.15 -> 0.47): the transform loses accuracy as diffusion grows.
QLBM is non-convergent at nu<=0.02 (relL2 ~ 0.8-1.0, error ~ signal) then snaps to single-digit-% error: it needs diffusion to be stable.
Complementary, not competitive: there is no nu where one method dominates the whole range -- the 'best tool' depends on the regime.
